message:
chore: refine reality-check beads after plan-space review

Fixes found during refinement:
- y17.1: Addressed config shape mismatch between README's flat format and
  code's nested types. Added to_internal() conversion. Dropped email field
  (out of scope). Specified env var security pattern for secrets.
- y17.2/y17.3: Clarified env var security pattern, removed duplicate tests
  (consolidated into y17.9)
- y17.4: Added specifics about config-to-internal mapping layer
- y17.5: Fixed the 24h counter bug — added reset_if_stale() method for
  MetricsSnapshot that resets counters older than 24h
- y17.6: Resolved open question: YES to 'serve' subcommand. Also adding
  'status --format prometheus' as simpler alternative.
- y17.7: Committed to builder pattern for watchdog config
- y17.9: Consolidated ALL notification tests (10 unit + 1 E2E)
- y17.10: Removed overlap with existing tests, focused on new functionality

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -27,13 +27,13 @@
 {"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","title":"task: add end-to-end test that runs 3-5 real ACFS installers and verifies results","description":"Add a bash E2E test script that exercises the full workflow:

1. Build the release binary
2. Run: afsc validate (should pass cleanly, zero warnings)
3. Run: afsc check zoxide atuin --parallel 2 --timeout 120
   - These are small, fast, reliable installers
   - Verify exit code 0
   - Verify human output shows 2 passed
4. Run: afsc check zoxide --format jsonl
   - Verify JSONL output is valid JSON per line
   - Verify status field is pass
   - Verify checksum_result.matches is true (SHA256 verification worked)
5. Run: afsc status
   - Verify it shows the most recent run (not No runs recorded yet)
   - Verify it lists the installers tested
6. Run: afsc list --format json
   - Verify all 41 installers are present
   - Verify each has url and sha256
7. Run: afsc check --local zoxide
   - Verify --local mode also works (no Docker needed for this step)
8. Verify exit code: run afsc check with a deliberately broken URL, verify non-zero exit

This test proves the entire tool works end-to-end against real ACFS data.
Add to scripts/e2e/tests/ alongside existing E2E tests.

Each step should log clearly: echo "=== STEP N: description ===" so failures are
easy to locate in CI logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:31.528025958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T22:23:14.954394571Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T22:23:14.954103426Z","close_reason":"Added real_installer_run.sh E2E test: validates checksums.yaml, dry-run check with --local, JSONL format verification, status command. Gated by ACFS repo availability.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:31.528025958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:31.926021874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:09:42.442954470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:44:36.170837111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:20:36.313989653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:32.847319353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:32.930595230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:33.318333044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:29:07.861534576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:20:33.107801052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","title":"task: update README to accurately reflect implemented vs planned features","description":"After all implementation is complete, do a final pass over README.md to ensure
every claim is accurate. The current README was written before the reality check and describes
some features as working that were actually stubs. Once the stubs are implemented, the README
should be accurate — but verify each claim against the actual code.

Specifically verify:
- Docker isolation claim matches real container usage
- Parallel execution claim matches real worker pool
- Error classification categories match the actual classifier
- Configuration options all actually work
- All command examples produce the shown output
- Architecture diagram matches the real code flow

This should be the LAST task — everything else must be done first.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:34.181570515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T22:25:22.094993595Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T22:25:22.094685889Z","close_reason":"Updated README: added --local flag to examples and limitations, reflecting all implemented features.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:34.181570515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:09:42.504499048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:09:43.237281383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:09:43.300772193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:29:07.976079110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:09:43.367712560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:44:54.241485328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:29:08.031633182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:29:08.087523192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-74o.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:25:34.576331856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","title":"epic: close all reality-check gaps — config schema, notifications, metrics, README accuracy","description":"Reality check (2026-04-06) found 5 gaps between README promises and actual implementation:

1. CONFIG SCHEMA GAP: README documents [notifications], [monitoring], and [watchdog] config sections that don't exist in src/config/schema.rs. A user following the README config example will get a toml parse error.

2. NOTIFICATIONS STUB: src/reporting/notify.rs has types but create_github_issue() and send_slack_message() are placeholders. Not wired into main.rs.

3. METRICS NOT WIRED: src/reporting/metrics.rs has MetricsSnapshot and MetricsExporter with 4 passing tests, but metrics are NEVER collected during check runs, and no HTTP server exposes /health or /metrics.

4. WATCHDOG CONFIG: SystemdWatchdog works via env vars, but README documents a [watchdog] config section that doesn't exist in schema.

5. README ACCURACY: Config example shows sections that silently fail or error.

Strategy: Config sections first (needed by everything else) -> notifications -> metrics -> README.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:21:41.218742251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:21:41.218742251Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reality-check"]}
-{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.1","title":"task: add [notifications], [monitoring], [watchdog] config sections to schema.rs","description":"README documents three config sections that don't exist in src/config/schema.rs:

[notifications]
  enabled: bool (default false)
  slack_webhook: String (webhook URL, default empty)
  github_issue_repo: String (e.g. 'owner/repo', default empty)
  email: String (default empty)

[monitoring]
  health_endpoint: bool (default false)
  health_port: u16 (default 8080)
  metrics_enabled: bool (default false)
  metrics_port: u16 (default 9090)

[watchdog]
  default_interval_seconds: u64 (default 120)
  log_pings: bool (default false)

Implementation:
1. Add NotificationsConfig, MonitoringConfig, WatchdogConfig structs to src/config/schema.rs
2. Add them as fields on the root Config struct (with #[serde(default)] so existing configs without these sections still load)
3. Update the test_load_config_from_file test to include the new sections
4. Add a test that verifies a config with all sections including the new ones loads correctly
5. Add a test that verifies a config WITHOUT the new sections still loads (backwards compat)

The #[serde(default)] attribute is CRITICAL — without it, existing config files that omit these sections will fail to parse. Every new field and every new section struct needs a Default impl.

This unblocks ALL other beads in this epic since notifications and metrics code needs to read config values.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-04-07T01:21:59.172565869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:21:59.172565869Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:21:59.172565869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.10","title":"task: add unit tests for metrics collection wiring","description":"Test coverage for the metrics collection wiring:

1. test_monitoring_config_defaults: verify MonitoringConfig defaults
2. test_watchdog_config_defaults: verify WatchdogConfig defaults
3. test_metrics_snapshot_persists_across_runs: create snapshot, record tests, save, load, verify counts
4. test_full_config_with_all_sections: parse a complete config.toml with all 7 sections

Most of the MetricsSnapshot logic is already tested (4 existing tests). These tests verify
the CONFIG wiring and the integration between cmd_check and metrics collection.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":20,"created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:23.953132525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:45.439377500Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.10","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:23.953132525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.10","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:45.438867515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.2","title":"task: implement real GitHub issue creation in notify.rs","description":"Replace the placeholder in src/reporting/notify.rs:61 create_github_issue() with a real implementation.

Current code (line 61-64):
  async fn create_github_issue(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str) -> Result<()> {
      tracing::info!("Would create GitHub issue");
      Ok(())
  }

Implementation:
1. Read the GitHub token from the env var specified in GitHubConfig.token_env
2. Use reqwest to POST to https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues
3. Request body: {"title": title, "body": body, "labels": ["automated-check"]}
4. Headers: Authorization: Bearer {token}, Accept: application/vnd.github+json, User-Agent: afsc
5. Handle errors gracefully: log warning on failure, don't crash the whole run
6. Handle missing token: if env var is not set, log info and skip (don't error)

The reqwest crate is already a dependency. No new dependencies needed.

Add tests:
- test_github_issue_skips_without_token: verify no panic when token env var is unset
- test_github_config_defaults: verify config struct has reasonable defaults","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:19.941220224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:36.631821222Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["notifications"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.2","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:19.941220224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.2","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:36.631290779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.3","title":"task: implement real Slack webhook notification in notify.rs","description":"Replace the placeholder in src/reporting/notify.rs:67 send_slack_message() with a real implementation.

Current code (line 67-70):
  async fn send_slack_message(&self, _title: &str, _message: &str) -> Result<()> {
      tracing::info!("Would send Slack message");
      Ok(())
  }

Implementation:
1. Read the webhook URL from the env var specified in SlackConfig.webhook_url_env
2. POST JSON to the webhook URL: {"text": "*{title}*\n{message}", "channel": channel}
3. For failure notifications, use red color attachment; for success, green
4. Handle errors gracefully: log warning on failure, don't crash
5. Handle missing webhook: if env var is not set, log info and skip

reqwest is already available. Slack webhook is just a simple POST.

Add tests:
- test_slack_skips_without_webhook: verify no panic when webhook env var is unset
- test_slack_config_defaults: verify config struct","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:20.865858128Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:36.708994990Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["notifications"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.3","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:20.865858128Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.3","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:36.708491287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.4","title":"task: wire notifications into check command failure/success path","description":"After test results are collected in cmd_check() (src/main.rs), send notifications if enabled.

Implementation in cmd_check() after the results summary and before process exit:
1. Read NotificationsConfig from the loaded config
2. If notifications.enabled is false, skip entirely
3. Construct a summary message: 'AFSC Check: X passed, Y failed out of Z total'
4. For failures: include the first 3 failure names and error categories
5. Create Notifier with the config and call notifier.notify(title, message, any_failed)
6. Log any notification errors but don't fail the check command

The Notifier already handles routing to GitHub/Slack based on config. We just need to:
- Import Notifier and NotificationConfig
- Construct the message
- Call notify()

This should be a small addition (~20 lines) to the existing cmd_check function.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:51.237054705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:37.718610543Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["notifications"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.4","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:51.237054705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.4","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:37.644427915Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.4","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:37.718120906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.5","title":"task: collect MetricsSnapshot during check runs and persist to disk","description":"MetricsSnapshot exists with record_test(), record_remediation(), save(), load() — all tested.
But it's NEVER called from cmd_check().

Implementation:
1. After test results are collected in cmd_check(), load or create a MetricsSnapshot:
   - Path: ~/.local/share/afsc/metrics.json (same base dir as results)
   - If file exists, load it. Otherwise, create default.
2. For each test result, call snapshot.record_test(result.success)
3. If remediation was attempted, call snapshot.record_remediation()
4. Save the updated snapshot back to disk
5. Log the updated metrics at info level

This gives us persistent metrics across runs without needing an HTTP server.
The /health and /metrics HTTP endpoints are a separate bead.

This is simple wiring — the MetricsSnapshot API already exists and is tested.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:51.952440416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:38.599425479Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["metrics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.5","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:51.952440416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.5","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:38.598903582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.6","title":"task: add optional HTTP health and metrics endpoints","description":"README promises [monitoring] config with health_endpoint and metrics_enabled flags.
When enabled, expose two HTTP endpoints that systemd or Prometheus can scrape.

Implementation:
1. When monitoring.health_endpoint is true, spawn an async HTTP server on monitoring.health_port
2. GET /health returns JSON: {status: 'ok', last_run: timestamp, success_rate_24h: float}
   sourced from the persisted MetricsSnapshot
3. When monitoring.metrics_enabled is true, also serve GET /metrics on monitoring.metrics_port
   (or same port, different path) returning Prometheus text format from MetricsExporter.export()
4. Use a minimal HTTP approach — hyper is already a transitive dependency via reqwest.
   Use axum or a bare hyper server (check what's available without adding deps).
   Actually, tokio + hyper are both present. A bare hyper service is ~40 lines.
5. The server should run in the background during long-running check sessions or as a
   standalone mode for systemd timer integration.

This is P3 because the tool works fine without it — it's useful for long-running scheduled checks.

Alternative: Instead of a persistent HTTP server, a simpler approach is to have the status command
serve the same data. The HTTP server is only needed for Prometheus scraping and systemd health
checks during long-running scheduled operations.

Consider: should this be a separate subcommand? E.g., 'afsc serve --health --metrics'?
That avoids complicating the check command with background server lifecycle.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:52.847503458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:39.219683998Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["metrics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.6","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:52.847503458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.6","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:39.219196355Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.7","title":"task: wire [watchdog] config section into SystemdWatchdog initialization","description":"The SystemdWatchdog in src/watchdog.rs currently auto-detects everything from env vars
(WATCHDOG_USEC, NOTIFY_SOCKET). The README documents a [watchdog] config section with:
  default_interval_seconds: 120
  log_pings: false

After the config schema bead adds WatchdogConfig to the schema, wire it in:

1. In main.rs where SystemdWatchdog::new() is called (line ~152), pass the config values:
   - Use watchdog.default_interval_seconds as the fallback interval when WATCHDOG_USEC is not set
   - Use watchdog.log_pings to control whether pings are logged at info (true) or trace (false)
2. This may require adding parameters to SystemdWatchdog::new() or a builder pattern
3. The env var detection should still take priority — config values are fallbacks

Small change, ~15 lines. Just wiring config into the existing working watchdog.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":20,"created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:21.352147862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:39.291026489Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["watchdog"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.7","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:21.352147862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.7","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:39.290496977Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.8","title":"task: update README config example and feature table to match implementation","description":"After all implementation beads are complete, update README.md to accurately reflect what works.

Changes needed:
1. Config example: should only show sections that actually parse and do something.
   After the config schema bead, all sections should work, but verify each one.
2. Feature table: update the 'What It Does' column if any features changed scope
   (e.g., if HTTP endpoints become a separate 'serve' subcommand).
3. Limitations section: remove items that are no longer limitations.
4. Add the new --local flag to the Quick Example if not already there.
5. Verify the 'Development' section's test count matches reality (now 357+ tests).
6. Verify all CLI examples still work.

+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.1","title":"task: add [notifications], [monitoring], [watchdog] config sections to schema.rs","description":"README documents three config sections that don't exist in src/config/schema.rs. This bead adds them WITH proper mapping to existing internal types.

CRITICAL DESIGN DECISION — Config shape vs internal types:
The README shows a flat config:
  [notifications]
  enabled = false
  slack_webhook = ""
  github_issue_repo = ""
  email = ""

But the existing notify.rs code has nested types:
  NotificationConfig { github: Option<GitHubConfig>, slack: Option<SlackConfig> }
  GitHubConfig { repo, token_env, create_issues, add_comments }
  SlackConfig { webhook_url_env, channel, notify_on_failure, notify_on_success }

RESOLUTION: The config TOML struct should be user-friendly (flat, matching README).
A conversion method maps it to the internal NotificationConfig for the Notifier.

SECURITY: Secrets (GitHub token, Slack webhook URL) should NOT be stored in config TOML.
Config stores the ENV VAR NAMES that hold the secrets, not the secrets themselves.
  slack_webhook_env = "SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL"  (env var name, not the URL)
  github_token_env = "GITHUB_TOKEN"        (env var name, not the token)
  github_issue_repo = "owner/repo"         (not a secret, can be in config)

EMAIL: README lists email = "" but no email implementation exists or is planned.
Remove email from the config schema and update README to drop it. Email notifications
are out of scope for a CLI tool — Slack and GitHub cover the notification use cases.

Implementation in src/config/schema.rs:

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct NotificationsConfig {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub enabled: bool,                          // default false
    #[serde(default)]
    pub slack_webhook_env: String,              // env var holding webhook URL
    #[serde(default)]
    pub slack_channel: String,                  // default empty (uses webhook default)
    #[serde(default)]
    pub github_token_env: String,               // env var holding GH token
    #[serde(default)]
    pub github_issue_repo: String,              // 'owner/repo' format
    #[serde(default = "default_true")]
    pub notify_on_failure: bool,                // default true
    #[serde(default)]
    pub notify_on_success: bool,                // default false
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct MonitoringConfig {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub health_endpoint: bool,                  // default false
    #[serde(default = "default_health_port")]
    pub health_port: u16,                       // default 8080
    #[serde(default)]
    pub metrics_enabled: bool,                  // default false
    #[serde(default = "default_metrics_port")]
    pub metrics_port: u16,                      // default 9090
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct WatchdogConfig {
    #[serde(default = "default_watchdog_interval")]
    pub default_interval_seconds: u64,          // default 120
    #[serde(default)]
    pub log_pings: bool,                        // default false
}

Add all three to Config with #[serde(default)] on each field.
Add a method: NotificationsConfig::to_internal() -> notify::NotificationConfig
that maps the flat config to the internal Notifier types, reading env vars at conversion time.

Also export the new types from config/mod.rs.

Tests:
1. test_config_with_all_sections: full TOML with all 7 sections parses correctly
2. test_config_without_new_sections: existing TOML without new sections still parses (backwards compat)
3. test_notifications_config_defaults: verify all defaults are sensible
4. test_monitoring_config_defaults: verify port defaults
5. test_watchdog_config_defaults: verify interval default","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-07T01:21:59.172565869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:33:29.753792507Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.1","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:21:59.172565869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.2","title":"task: implement real GitHub issue creation in notify.rs","description":"Replace the placeholder in src/reporting/notify.rs create_github_issue() with a real implementation.

Current code (line 61-64):
  async fn create_github_issue(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str) -> Result<()> {
      tracing::info!("Would create GitHub issue");
      Ok(())
  }

SECURITY PATTERN: The GitHub token comes from an ENV VAR (e.g. GITHUB_TOKEN), never from
the config file. The config only stores the env var NAME (github_token_env) and the repo.

Implementation:
1. Read the GitHub token: std::env::var(&self.config.github.token_env)
2. If env var is not set or empty, log info and return Ok(()) (graceful skip)
3. Parse the repo into owner/repo from self.config.github.repo
4. Use reqwest to POST to https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues
5. Request body: {"title": title, "body": body, "labels": ["afsc-automated"]}
6. Headers: Authorization: Bearer {token}, Accept: application/vnd.github+json, User-Agent: afsc/0.1
7. Check response status: 201 = success, log the issue URL
8. On HTTP error: log warning with status code and response body excerpt, return Ok(())
9. On network error: log warning, return Ok(())

CRITICAL: Never propagate errors. Notification failures must NEVER cause the check command to fail.

The Notifier needs to be updated to accept the NEW config shape from y17.1's
NotificationsConfig.to_internal() conversion, not the old hardcoded internal shape.

No tests in THIS bead — tests are consolidated in y17.9 to avoid duplication.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:19.941220224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:33:45.658465988Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["notifications"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.2","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:19.941220224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.2","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:36.631290779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.3","title":"task: implement real Slack webhook notification in notify.rs","description":"Replace the placeholder in src/reporting/notify.rs send_slack_message() with a real implementation.

Current code (line 67-70):
  async fn send_slack_message(&self, _title: &str, _message: &str) -> Result<()> {
      tracing::info!("Would send Slack message");
      Ok(())
  }

SECURITY PATTERN: The webhook URL comes from an ENV VAR (e.g. SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL), never
stored in config TOML. Config only stores the env var NAME (slack_webhook_env) and optional channel.

Implementation:
1. Read the webhook URL: std::env::var(&self.config.slack.webhook_url_env)
2. If env var is not set or empty, log info and return Ok(()) (graceful skip)
3. Build the Slack payload. For failures, use attachment with color #ff0000 (red):
   {"channel": channel_or_default, "attachments": [{"color": color, "title": title, "text": message}]}
   For success, color #36a64f (green).
4. POST to webhook URL with Content-Type: application/json
5. Check response: 200 = success. Anything else: log warning with response body.
6. On network error: log warning, return Ok(())

CRITICAL: Never propagate errors. Notification failures must NEVER cause the check command to fail.

+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.4","title":"task: wire notifications into check command failure/success path","description":"Wire notifications into the check command's failure/success path in src/main.rs.

After test results are collected in cmd_check(), and after result persistence, send notifications.

Implementation:
1. Read config.notifications (the new NotificationsConfig from y17.1)
2. If !config.notifications.enabled, skip entirely (early return, no object construction)
3. Convert config to internal type: config.notifications.to_internal()
   This reads env vars for secrets at this point (not at config load time)
4. Create Notifier from the internal config
5. Build summary message:
   Title: 'AFSC: {passed}/{total} passed' or 'AFSC: {failed} failures in {total} tests'
   Body: List up to 5 failure names with their error categories.
   Include run_id and timestamp for traceability.
6. Call notifier.notify(title, body, any_failed).await
7. If notify returns Err, log it at warn level but DO NOT propagate — the check command
   must exit based on test results, not notification success.

Location in main.rs: after the 'Persist results to JSONL file' block, before the exit(1) check.
This ensures results are saved even if notification hangs/fails.

+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.5","title":"task: collect MetricsSnapshot during check runs and persist to disk","description":"Collect MetricsSnapshot during check runs and persist to disk.

MetricsSnapshot exists with record_test(), record_remediation(), save(), load() — all tested.
But it is NEVER called from cmd_check(). This bead wires it in.

KNOWN ISSUE TO FIX: The 24h counters (total_tests_24h, successful_tests_24h, success_rate_24h)
never reset. record_test() just increments forever. Fix this:
1. When loading an existing snapshot, check snapshot_time
2. If snapshot_time is older than 24h, reset the 24h counters to zero before recording
3. Add a method: MetricsSnapshot::reset_if_stale() that does this check
4. Call it immediately after load(), before recording new results

Implementation in cmd_check():
1. Determine metrics path: ~/.local/share/afsc/metrics.json (same parent as results/)
2. Load existing snapshot, or create default if file doesn't exist
3. Call snapshot.reset_if_stale() to handle 24h window
4. For each test result: snapshot.record_test(result.success)
5. If remediation was attempted (remediate flag && any_failed): snapshot.record_remediation()
6. Save snapshot back to disk
7. Log at debug level: 'Metrics updated: {total_tests_24h} tests, {success_rate_24h:.1}% success rate'

This does NOT depend on MonitoringConfig — metrics are always collected (it's cheap, just a JSON file).
The MonitoringConfig only controls whether HTTP endpoints EXPOSE the metrics.

Add to MetricsSnapshot:
  pub fn reset_if_stale(&mut self) {
      let age = Utc::now() - self.snapshot_time;
      if age > chrono::Duration::hours(24) {
          self.total_tests_24h = 0;
          self.successful_tests_24h = 0;
          self.success_rate_24h = 0.0;
          self.total_remediations_24h = 0;
      }
  }

Estimated ~20 lines in cmd_check() + 10 lines for reset_if_stale().","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":35,"created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:51.952440416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:34:35.815797555Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["metrics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.5","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:51.952440416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.5","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:38.598903582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.6","title":"task: add optional HTTP health and metrics endpoints","description":"Add optional HTTP health and metrics endpoints via a 'serve' subcommand.

DESIGN DECISION (resolved in plan space): This is a SEPARATE SUBCOMMAND, not embedded in check.
Rationale: check exits after running tests; a persistent HTTP server needs to run continuously.
systemd would run 'afsc serve' as a long-running service while 'afsc check' runs on a timer.

New CLI command:
  automated_flywheel_setup_checker serve [--health-port 8080] [--metrics-port 9090]

Behavior:
1. Read MonitoringConfig from config.toml
2. If health_endpoint is false AND metrics_enabled is false, print error and exit
3. Start a minimal HTTP server using hyper (transitive dep of reqwest, already available)
4. Routes:
   GET /health -> JSON {"status": "ok", "last_run": ..., "success_rate_24h": ..., ...}
     Reads MetricsSnapshot from the persisted ~/.local/share/afsc/metrics.json
   GET /metrics -> Prometheus text format from MetricsExporter
     Same data, different format
5. Server runs until SIGTERM/SIGINT (integrate with systemd watchdog if --watchdog)
6. If the metrics.json file doesn't exist yet, /health returns {"status": "no_data"}

Implementation approach:
- Use hyper::Server directly (it's already in the dependency tree via reqwest)
- Single port serving both routes (simplest). Use health_port as the listen port.
- ~80 lines: service function with match on path, read snapshot, format response
- Add 'Serve' variant to Commands enum in main.rs

ALTERNATIVE (simpler, for users without Prometheus):
  automated_flywheel_setup_checker status --format prometheus
  This outputs Prometheus text format to stdout. Prometheus can use exec or textfile collector.
  Implement THIS TOO as a quick addition to the existing status command — it's just a new
  output format branch using MetricsExporter.export(). ~15 lines.

Tests:
- test_serve_command_help: verify CLI parses the serve subcommand
- test_prometheus_format_output: verify MetricsExporter.export() produces valid Prometheus text
  (this can be a unit test without an HTTP server)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:52.847503458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:35:05.398666413Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["metrics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.6","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:22:52.847503458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.6","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:39.219196355Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.7","title":"task: wire [watchdog] config section into SystemdWatchdog initialization","description":"Wire [watchdog] config section into SystemdWatchdog initialization.

The SystemdWatchdog in src/watchdog.rs auto-detects from env vars (WATCHDOG_USEC, NOTIFY_SOCKET).
After y17.1 adds WatchdogConfig to the schema, wire the config values as fallbacks.

Implementation:
1. Add a builder method to SystemdWatchdog:
   pub fn with_config(mut self, config: &WatchdogConfig) -> Self {
       if self.interval.is_none() {
           self.interval = Some(Duration::from_secs(config.default_interval_seconds));
       }
       self.log_pings = config.log_pings;
       self
   }
2. In main.rs where SystemdWatchdog::new() is called (~line 152):
   let wd = Arc::new(SystemdWatchdog::new().with_config(&config.watchdog));
3. In the ping loop: if log_pings is true, log at info level; if false, log at trace level
4. Env var WATCHDOG_USEC still takes priority over config (it's the systemd contract)

This requires adding 'interval: Option<Duration>' and 'log_pings: bool' fields to SystemdWatchdog
if they don't already exist. Check the current struct fields before implementing.

+{"id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.9","title":"task: add unit tests for notification wiring and config","description":"Comprehensive test coverage for the ENTIRE notification pipeline (config -> Notifier -> GitHub/Slack).

This is the SINGLE bead for all notification tests. y17.2 and y17.3 do NOT include their own tests
to avoid duplication.

Unit tests (no actual HTTP calls):
1. test_notifications_config_defaults: NotificationsConfig defaults (enabled=false, empty env var names)
2. test_notifications_config_from_toml: parse TOML with notification settings, verify all fields
3. test_notifications_config_to_internal: verify to_internal() conversion maps fields correctly
4. test_notifier_skips_when_disabled: create Notifier with enabled=false, call notify(), verify returns Ok
5. test_github_skips_missing_token: GitHubConfig present but env var not set -> graceful skip, no panic
6. test_slack_skips_missing_webhook: SlackConfig present but env var not set -> graceful skip, no panic
7. test_github_skips_empty_repo: github_issue_repo is empty string -> skip, don't crash
8. test_slack_skips_empty_env: slack_webhook_env is empty string -> skip, don't crash
9. test_notify_on_failure_only: notify_on_failure=true, notify_on_success=false, call with success=true -> verify Slack NOT called
10. test_notify_on_success: notify_on_success=true, call with success=true -> verify Slack WOULD be called (check log output or mock)

Integration test (E2E script):
11. Add to scripts/e2e/tests/notification_config.sh:
    - Build binary, create config with [notifications] section, run check --dry-run
    - Verify the config parses without error (exit 0)
    - Verify a config with ONLY [notifications] section (no other sections) also parses (tests serde(default))

All tests must work WITHOUT real GitHub/Slack credentials — test the ROUTING logic and graceful degradation, not the HTTP calls.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:22.925341283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:35:37.741663082Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:22.925341283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.9","depends_on_id":"automated_flywheel_setup_checker-y17.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:23:44.520368265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
